workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Can you extract the escalation triage from this email thread for Evergreen Solutions? This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 17:19 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Evergreen Solutions escalation triage

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with escalation triage. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-01-03 12:47 EST
Subject: Re: Re: escalation triage Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Evergreen Solutions: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-L trap (Proposed pipeline note duplicates content already in MSX); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-L failure mode (Proposed pipeline note duplicates content already in MSX)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-L -- Proposed pipeline note duplicates content already in MSX
